programmes.show_description: The Resident Patient. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read in which Blessington seeks help from Sherlock Holmes after discovering intruders have been in his bedroom.
And in Again. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie gives Charlie Endell a birthday present, but Charlie shows his appreciation in a very peculiar way.
1938. War. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars Barry K. Barnes, Sophie Stewart & Jack Hawkins. During World War One, a number of captured British officers attempt to escape a prisoner-of-war camp.
All Good Friends: The Case of the Handless Corpse. 1992. Stars Suzanne Packer & Michelle Chadwick. Based on the 1979 case, where two divers found the body of a man in a flooded Lancashire quarry.
1965. A Million Bucks In Anybody's Language. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. Honey investigates the death of a P.I.
Deputy Sheriff. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Maurice Marsac & Celia Lovsky. Jim volunteers to become a deputy sheriff to protect a prisoner, the son of a French society member, from mob violence.
1937. Drama. Director: Syd Courtenay. Stars Peggy Simpson, Ian Fleming & Tod Slaughter. A story focusing on a woman's passion for an idle man and her marriage to his uncle, the family benefactor.
1951. Mystery. Directed by Francis Searle. Stars Brian Reece, Joy Shelton & Christine Norden. PC 49 teams up with his girlfriend to solve a crime after a murder is committed right under his nose.
1952. Comedy. In this British classic, the Pickwick Club send a group to find interesting things around England. Stars James Hayter, Nigel Patrick, Kathleen Harrison & Harry Fowler.
A short travelogue describing the history of Kent in 1954, with a look at the towns that made up the Cinque Ports.
1963. Crime Drama. Director: Lance Comfort. When writer Greg Parker (Tony Britton) goes on holiday, little does he expect to be lodging in the same B&B as an escaped murderer (William Lucas).
The Emperor's Hat. 1958. Stars Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. William is forced to show his renowned marksmanship with the crossbow by shooting an apple off his son's head.
1960. Comedy. Directed by Ralph Thomas. Stars Leslie Phillips, Joan Sims & Fenella Fielding. Richard Hare's love life has as many ups and downs, as does his career.
1959. The Ghost Train Murder. Directed by Geoffrey Muller. Starring Russell Napier, Gorgon Needham and Mary Laura Wood. A Hungarian girl is found murdered; stabbed by knitting needles. (S1, E31)
1940. The ministry of supply launches a nationwide campaign to prevent at home waste burning and encourage sorting of waste. This rubbish can be reused for a profit.
1948. Drama. Directors: Ken Annakin & Michael C Chorlton. Stars Phyllis Calvert, James Donald & David Tomlinson. A plane crash lands miles from civilisation. The passengers must prevail.
Episode in Bathurst. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Chuck Faulkner & Joe McCormick. Cobb's stages are halted outside Bathurst until he agrees to pay toll to gunmen who have taken over the town.
The Dove Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo steals a dove-shaped medal from the leader of a European nation. The medal has information about Thrush.
The Reluctant Witness. Stars John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. Some neighborhood thugs who run a stolen car ring beat a man to death. A timid shop girl is the only witness.
Murders in Batz - Les Blessures de l¿île. 2014. Director: Edwin Baily. Stars Stéphane Freiss & Flore Bonaventura. An inspector investigates the island's first murder in a century as storm hits.
Join the fabulous Caroline Munro down in the Cellar Club. Tonight, we start with The World of Hammer episode Dracula and the Undead, which explores classic Hammer films featuring Dracula.
Dracula and the Undead. 1994. Narrated by Oliver Reed. A look at how Hammer defined Dracula as a seductive icon of forbidden desire and everlasting evil with clips from a number of classic films.
1959. Horror. Directed by Bernard L. Kowalski. Stars Ken Clark, Yvette Vickers & Jan Shepard. A man discovers giant leeches are behind several recent disappearances, but will anyone believe him?
Dreaming of Thee. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie's 3 weeks from release from prison with time off for good behaviour. However, Charles Endell visits him on visiting day.
1970. Comedy. Director: Brian De Palma. Stars Robert De Niro & Allen Garfield. A Vietnam veteran returns to New York and begins filmmaking, but he soon falls in with a group of radical activists.
A rare treat, courtesy of a Dutch airline, about air travel in the 1960s.
To Wear a Badge. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E10)
Good Deed. Stars Keefe Brasselle, Jay C. Flippen & James Best. British actor David Niven, introduces this 1950s US anthology series. (S1, E12)
Granny Versus the Weather Bureau. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, John McGiver & Quinn O'Hara. Granny's old-fashioned ways of predicting the weather are pitted against the Weather Bureau's technology.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life One for the Road
programmes.show_description: 1965. A thought provoking look at the problem of drinking and driving and how it is being tackled.
1942. Mystery. Director: Edward Dmytryk. Stars Chester Morris, Harriet Hilliard & Richard Lane. A reformed thief becomes involved in a case to find a woman's missing sculpture of Augustus Caesar.
1948. Crime Drama starring John Bentley & Dinah Sheridan. Scotland Yard is unable to unravel the 'Rex' murders and cannot manage without the help of Paul Temple and his sidekick 'Steve'.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Where Have All...
programmes.show_description: ... the Butterflies Gone? 1968. An enchanting look into the natural world of the butterfly and the reasons for the apparent shortage of them.
1959. Drama. Director: Godfrey Grayson. Stars Sean Lynch, Beth Rogan & Raymond Huntley. A teenager is falsely accused of robbery. A young girl must persuade him to give himself up.
1949. Comedy. Directed by & stars Robert Donat. Features Reneé Asherson & Thord Hird. A sergeant returns from the war and tries to reconcile with his fiancée but winds up falling for a new lodger.
1961. Crime Drama. Directed by Michael Winner. Stars Terence Longden, Donald Gray and Diane Clare. A reporter learns that his brother, a student at Cambridge, has killed himself or so it seems.
Blackbeard. 1956. Stars Alec Clunes, George Margo & Peter Hammond. Blackbeard becomes suspicious when a new governor arrives in the Bahamas and offers the pirates pardons from the King.
The Knock on the door. 1976. Stars Jean Anderson, Patrick O'Connell & Richard Easton. Brian rejects Jane's budget, Barbara questions Brian on his divorce and Gwen gets a visit from the police.
Illegal Entry. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Walter Gotell & Adrienne Corri. Maguire gains insight into a recent wave of robberies after a man is arrested for skulking around on the roof of a shop.
1964. Thriller. Director: Bryan Forbes. Stars Richard Attenborough, Nanette Newman & Kim Stanley. A medium stages a kidnapping which she will then 'solve' by pretending to be psychic.
A short travelogue describing the history of Kent in 1954, with a look at the towns that made up the Cinque Ports.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. Narrated by Richard Todd, looking at the life of James Justice Robertson.
Pannage/Dry Fly Fishing on the River Test. 1981. Jack Hargreaves watches woodland pigs foraging for acorns at pannage time. He then continues his album of dry fly fishing on the River Test.
1957. The White Cliffs Mystery. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Starring Russell Napier, Yvonne Andre and Peter Bathurst. A dead rocket engineer was being blackmailed. But why - and by whom? (S1, E25)
A Kind of Cuckoo. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Shirley Anne Field & Clifford Rose. Monica digs into Burton's past and the new board clashes over control, cash and contracts.
1949. Drama. Directed by Sidney Cole, Charles Crichton and Basil Dearden. Starring Jack Warner, Susan Shaw and Gladys Henson. A train disaster is told as four short stories.
1988. Crime Drama. Directed by Colin Luke. Stars Amanda Donohoe, Stephen Dillane & Hilary Mason. A young novelist falls for a married woman, who then wants him to kill her husband.
